text:
All the animals lived happily ever after.

There once was a peculiar little rabbit who loved numbers. Every night she listened to the owls' bedtime stories about the adventures of the last lynx. Each story ended with the phrase: "And the owl flew away."

That night... she didnpeculiarly quiet.


The owl knew a thing or two:

The rabbits gathered 'round
With their ears wide awake.

One odd-looking bat took flight.
"You've got no tail," she sighed.

The owl smiled mischievously. "I've a heart big enough..."
And away she flew!

A lonely fox meandered down the hill.
"Bird? Sounds like trouble," he thought.

The owl swooped boldly. "I have secrets!
A tale about an old oak tree.
Old Oak never talks.
But listen hard, just wait."

"Why oak?" puzzled the fox.

"I'll tell you on a moonlit night,"...

So, on a beautiful moonlit night, the owl
